signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Intervention
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.73 — Strait of Hormuz closure and US-Iran tensions drive WTI above $99.0, supporting USD via energy-security flows. Despite BoJ Summary of Opinions showing hawkish shift, the +300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage sustains the bid. JPY COT LF net short -61,340 confirms crowded carry positioning. 160.00 remains the key level for BoJ intervention risk, reaffirmed by recent US-Japan currency coordination talks. Carry dominates until VIX > 25.

signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Positioning + Retail
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 114.001 — G8 spread of 1.034% highlights JPY underperformance vs AUD strength. AUD COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) indicates heavy institutional backing, while retail sentiment is 85% short. This extreme institutional vs retail divergence suggests retail capitulation risk if price extends higher. +360bp RBA-BoJ carry premium provides structural support. Watch for JPY safe-haven bid if VIX breaches 25.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5956 — COT LF net short -19,294 (18.5% long) shows specs heavily fading the currency despite a hawkish RBNZ market bias. With a +150bp Fed-NZD carry deficit, the pair remains pressured, but the extreme spec short positioning introduces significant short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ delivers a hike or hawkish guidance. Watch for spec long% moving toward 20% to signal a floor.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1759 — Correlation with EuroStoxx at +0.701 vs norm of -0.125 (z=+1.87σ) indicates EUR is trading as a pure risk-proxy rather than a funding currency. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (+175bp) carry advantage keeps upside structurally limited. COT LF net short -21,011 suggests specs are positioned for further downside ahead of US inflation data. Break of 1.1700 targets 1.1650.
